speaker: EPILOGUE
text: ’Tis ten to one this play can never please

speaker: EPILOGUE
text: All that are here. Some come to take their ease

speaker: EPILOGUE
text: And sleep an act or two—but those, we fear,

speaker: EPILOGUE
text: We’ve frighted with our trumpets; so, ’tis clear,

speaker: EPILOGUE
text: They’ll say ’tis naught—others, to hear the city

speaker: EPILOGUE
text: Abused extremely and to cry “That’s witty!”—

speaker: EPILOGUE
text: Which we have not done neither—that I fear

speaker: EPILOGUE
text: All the expected good we’re like to hear

speaker: EPILOGUE
text: For this play at this time is only in

speaker: EPILOGUE
text: The merciful construction of good women,

speaker: EPILOGUE
text: For such a one we showed ’em. If they smile

speaker: EPILOGUE
text: And say ’twill do, I know within a while

speaker: EPILOGUE
text: All the best men are ours; for ’tis ill hap

speaker: EPILOGUE
text: If they hold when their ladies bid ’em clap.
